title: hieracium dubium subsp. sysolskiense
intro: Hieracium dubium subsp. sysolskiense, also known as the lesser hawkbit, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in herbal medicine, valued for their astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Hieracium dubium subsp. sysolskiense are believed to provide several health benefits, including wound healing, reduction of inflammation, and antimicrobial action. This plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat minor ailments and promote overall well-being.
